title: NAME A MOVE
starterCode:
// You can make your OWN command! function stair() { ... } names a move.
// Then call it by name: stair()
hints: Look at the staircase: the same little move repeats — forward, turn, forward, turn back.
Give that move a name with function stair() { … }, then call stair() to use it.
You'll call stair() a few times to climb all the way. How many steps is the staircase?
briefing: Time to build your own commands. A function gives a name to a bunch of moves. Name the staircase step 'stair', then just call stair() to use it — again and again.
authorSolution:
function stair() {
  forward(1)
  right()
  forward(1)
  left()
}
stair()
stair()
stair()
